rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. MIKE O'REILLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Dan Nolting'S Motion For Dimsissal With Prejudice Pursuant To Ccp 581 And Monetary Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.5
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 24, 2013, Line 22, Defendant Dan Nolting'S Motion For Dismissal With Prejudice Pursuant To Ccp 581 And Monetary Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.5. Granted. On November 15, 2012, the court sustained, with 20 days leave to amend, Mr. Nolting's demurrer and granted in part his motion to strike. The Notice of Entry of Order of these rulings were served on November 19, 2012 by Defendant. As of December 18, 2012, Plaintiff had not contacted defendant's office that he was experiencing any hardships that would necessitate additional time to amend his complaint, nor did he request additional time to amend his Complaint either to defendant or the Court. Defendant's motion to dismiss set for hearing on January 7, 2013. Plaintiff did not file an opposition until January 4, 2013. The Court continued the hearing to January 17, 2013 on its own motion. Mr. Eng has not demonstrated good cause why he has not filed an amended complaint or why a further continuance of 45 days on this matter should be granted. Monetary sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ETHAN CREPS VS. TRUCO MARINE, LLC, AKA PATRIOT HOLDINGS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 24, 2013, Line 26, DEFENDANTs TRUE MARINE, LLC, AMERICAN SHIP MANAGEMENT, LLC & PATRIOT MARITIME COMPLIANCE, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication is denied. Defendants fail to show that they were operating the HAYES as agents of the United States under the SIAA and PVA. Defendants have failed to shift their burden on this defense. See evidence cited by Plaintiff in opposition to UMF 4, 5, 6, 7 and 23. Further, there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendants were plaintiff's employer. A seaman may have more than one employer. See Guidry v. South Louisiana Contractors, Inc., 614 F.2d 447, 452 (5th Cir. 1980). Plaintiff's affidavit (Hillsman Declaration, Ex. A) and attachments show that "Patriot" was the employer on the dispatch. "Patriot" is a vague reference that could refer to PCS or PMC. Ex. 2 to Kreps' affidavit shows that ASM issued the checks. At UMF 7, Defendants concede that PCS, Truco, PMC, and ASM are all affiliated entities that perform separate business functions. They all share the same office. In short, there is a triable issue of fact regarding the relationship among the entities, their relationship with plaintiff, and whether any can be considered plaintiff's employer or government agents for immunity under the SIAA and PVA. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: KURT A. TINDLE VS. GRANT YEE LEONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Attendance At Independent Medical Examination
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 24, 2013, at 9:00 am, Line 1, Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Attendance At Independent Medical Examination Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant GRANT YEE LEONG's Motion to Compel Attendance at IME is DENIED on the following grounds: 1) Notice does not state proper C.C.P. section for bringing motion for order compelling compliance with defendant's demand as required by CRC Rule 3.1110; 2) Defendant failed to submit evidence that Dr. Strassberg is a "licensed physician or other appropriate licensed health care practitioner," as required by C.C.P. section 2032.020; 3) Defendant's Request for IME dated October 15, 2012 does not comply with C.C.P. section 2032.220; and 4) No good cause shown that second IME of plaintiff is necessary. Defendant's request for sanctions is DENIED. Defendant's request for reimbursement of late fees is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: DINTCHO VS. O'BRIEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Milton O'Brien (Amended) Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 24, 2013, Line 25, Defendant Milton O'Brien's (Amended) Motion To Tax Costs. The motion to tax costs is granted as to Items g (attorney fees of $15,225) and h (fees under CCP § 685.070e for $100) in their entirety. The motion to tax costs is granted as to Item e (levying officer's fees) in the amount of $85. Regarding Item g, Plaintiff should have filed a noticed motion for attorney fees as required by CCP § 685.080. In any event, the motion and opposition make clear that Plaintiff is not entitled to attorney's fees under CCP § 685.040. The original judgment entered provided for attorney fees "incurred in enforcing the stipulated settlement." (Judgment dated 10/2/97.) The Judgment did not include an award of attorney's fees to the judgment creditor pursuant to CCP § 1033.5(a)(10). Neither did the Settlement Agreement attached to the Judgment. Regarding Item h, Plaintiff did not oppose the motion as it pertained to this item and Defendant correctly argues that these costs are already included at Item d. Regarding Item e, Plaintiff is not entitled to costs for attempting to execute against an entity which is not listed as a judgment debtor. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
